meta.title: Income Generating Strategies in Crypto: Real World Examples

transcript:
Pierre Henry: How you doing?

Evgeny Gokhberg: Good. Thank you for the kind introduction.

Pierre Henry: Yeah, for sure. Um, so again, it just I think starting at the at a high level is the best is the best way to do this. Um, what what even is yield in in crypto? And and sort of uh what is institutional grade yield uh especially vis-a-vis Solana because it feels like, you know, across markets and risk engines and and chains, everything is different. So that's my first question to you.

Evgeny Gokhberg: Yeah. So the the problem with crypto is everything means nothing and it means everything at the same time, right? So what is yield? Yield could be, you know, staking because you're underwriting security. It could be yield from lending, yield from market making or trading or what have you. But at an institutional level, if you're a TradFi allocator, yield is basically whatever you can, you know, package into that. So we've been running a market neutral DeFi yield fund for about five years now, basically delivering kind of market neutral returns every month. And institutions love that because it gives them an ability to squeeze out some value from the industry, to put it, you know, very bluntly, um, without taking any directional risk. And when it comes to Solana specifically, it's a very interesting chain to be discussing yield on because there are some chains that are built for, you know, slow moving and kind of capital preservation where, you know, like EVM and that sort of thing. And Solana has always been, you know, velocity, speculation and kind of movement rather than static. Um, but you obviously have the staking yield, which has been, you know, materially higher than for EVM. You have lending, so Kamino has done an amazing job with that. And then you have yields from, you know, JLP and other types of trading market making operations. And when you combine all of that into a portfolio of yields which are extremely diversified in the nature of the source of risk and the source of PnL, you can construct a pretty interesting portfolio.

Pierre Henry: Um, and and how how do you how what say more about that framework to assess the risk. I mean it feels like the yield on Solana has always been amazing, right? Especially if there's a falling rate environment, you have a gross yield around 8% usually. And I look at something like uh Ethereum or or or you know, Sui or one of these other uh L1s and it's much much lower. So is the is the risk higher on Solana or how how do you how do you measure that?

Evgeny Gokhberg: So one interesting thing is we've realized having built our own kind of DeFi risk management framework is that risk doesn't always correlate with the return. Quite often you have very low yielding platforms which are basically untouchable from a risk perspective and something else that is much safer gives you higher yield. But I think it's also important to separate the yield on the underlying crypto asset, you know, so staking uh for ETH or for SOL, and yields on stablecoins, right? So these are very different beasts. Um, obviously Solana and some of the other Proof of Stake, you know, PoS chains, they just have had higher inflation for a variety of reasons which frankly have nothing to do with their riskiness. It's just that's been the economic kind of inflation. And then when it comes to stablecoin yields, which you can then benchmark across all the chains, Solana is actually exactly on par. So we deploy uh we've deployed over a billion into kind of stablecoin yield strategies across pretty much every single chain and, you know, hundreds if not more uh probably thousands by now of protocols. And it's it's not like we can come to Solana and say, well, it's much riskier therefore we demand, you know, a 10% spread. You're getting the same rates as on EVM and it's all a function of the underlying protocol. And you can actually argue due to the conceptual nature and philosophy of how Solana is built, it's actually more secure because it's less open sourced.

Pierre Henry: Um, and so I guess like if you were speaking to uh an old world institution right now, where should they uh knowing their ICs and how they behave and everything, where should they actually uh spend time? Because I think what you're saying is, you know, there's also these huge drawdowns which happen. And so, you know, maybe the yield is great, but if you uh if you still have full exposure to the downside, is it even worth it at all? Maybe with the stablecoins it's a better way to go. What would you say to the institution to direct them?

Evgeny Gokhberg: I think the biggest problem with institutions is education or lack thereof and the career risk problem, right? So if you're an institution, you want to do the thing that is the most acceptable and not go beyond that because best case scenario you get a small bonus, worst case scenario you get fired, right? So people optimize around that. So we've been spending a lot of time with banks and other types of players explaining to them that uh, you know, the typical counterargument we get is we tell them, listen, there is great yield and it's amazing. And they're like, yeah, but you know, I'm on I'm taking crypto risk in exchange for 20% a year. And then I ask them to define what they mean by crypto risk. And they're like, well Bitcoin going down or like FTX. And then I explain that DeFi yields are completely different. The source of risk is software. And there is no other investment strategy in the world that pays you yield in exchange for taking software risk. So we've been educating people that they can actually build a risk management framework that is very very similar to fixed income. Where, you know, you give out a hundred loans and some of them might default, but the others that don't pay the yield and then statistically over the course of your portfolio you will hopefully be okay. And then if people are willing to take directional investments and they're paid to wait uh through positive carry on staking, that that's great. But those who just want high yields, they need to understand there is a risk management framework which is really really similar. And then you can deliver yields you can't get in TradFi with a risk that from a mathematical standpoint is lower than in TradFi.

Pierre Henry: Right. Um, and so just to be intellectually honest, I mean some of these L1s uh they do experience downtime. They they they do have uh or you know issues with oracles, they have issues where uh uh there there is a um a stress scenario of some sort, a crunch of liquidity. Uh how how uh you know, that's a very different series of uh considerations versus just creditor risk um and counterparty risk and things. So uh how how how does that work on Solana with your fund? I mean is is versus what, you know, an institution would do normally?

Evgeny Gokhberg: So I would argue that philosophically it's very similar to actual like credit or default risk. In the sense that you deploy your capital somewhere, you'll get paid whatever, 1% a year, um or you will lose everything in one go. And whether you lose everything in one go because of a corporate default or fraud or a hack, frankly doesn't matter. You still lose everything. So you can construct frameworks around that, but to your point, there are existential points of risk. So whenever you're doing anything in in DeFi to earn yield, you're taking the asset risk, stablecoin or SOL, you're taking the platform risk and the chain risk. So some chains uh have just been horrible, right? There's been, you know, like Fantom chain many years ago basically blew up because the underlying bridge that was backing it was a multisig and the Chinese police whatever arrested people who've been running it and the chain collapsed. So you need to take it into account because then well it's not a chain. Um, so you have these uh existential risks and you basically need to do your work and just avoid them and never touch them. Then you have things like Solana which are obviously incredible, you know, we're all here because we love Solana. But the say the chain has been halted a couple of times. And sometimes, you know, good luck getting a transaction through. So you need to kind of model for that in how you allocate risk because what if, you know, ADL is starting to happen and you need to urgently move your capital? Well, you need to be ready that maybe you will not be able to do that. And then you can only deploy certain types of strategies where the urgency of movement is less relevant or less likely to be required versus let's say Ethereum. Right? So you need to design the type of strategy as a function of the type of chain you're using because you need to be aware of these features and these risks because this is all software that's being tested in real time and you're getting paid pretty pretty insane yields, but risk is there and you need to know what you're doing.

Pierre Henry: Yeah, yeah. For sure. And uh I guess for for most institutions they're going to approach this uh looking at staking first, right? They're going to see, okay, ETFs, you know, that's something familiar to me, I can get it in my brokerage. They seem to embed staking. But there's also a timing component to that too, right? I mean in Solana's case, I think you can unlock within 24-48 hours, but that's not uh the case with Ethereum or some of some of the others out there. Um, so and and I guess uh how is this going to to to change over the next 12 to 18 months? You know, as as the as hopefully for Solana, right, it will be adopted. Uh everyone will participate in the ETF, people will realize this is a great uh uh opportunity and they'll all come in. Is is the yield going to collapse uh or or what will happen over time? How how do you think about that?

Evgeny Gokhberg: Well, so I think the starting point is if there is a business that can be commoditized away to zero over time, it will happen. So ETFs are a race to the bottom. I remember first reading about BlackRock's ETF with zero fees like I don't know 10 years ago and just being blown away by that. Uh custodians, their fees are being commoditized. So everyone needs to upsell, everyone needs to pur pursue and chase yield. So regulation has been the only blocker where no one is going to sign off on this until they have a green light from the relevant regulator, which is basically happening now already. So people will go into that and then these rates should go down because, you know, why should Solana be paying this much over the long term to maintain the security? Probably that number could be materially lower. But ultimately for these types of institutions, they um, you know, if they're they will not be investing in let's say SOL uh because they care about yield. Yeah. Because, you know, like today Solana is down 6% because people don't like what the Fed did. Uh I mean that's your year of yield gone in one morning. So the 99.99% of your return will come from price appreciation or depreciation, not the yield. So if they're investing, they're investing for the upside and yield is just a bonus. And if yield goes to zero, they don't care. And if it's an ETF product, they're selling access, so they don't care either. But as long as they can offer you at least 1%, they'll do it because it allows them to push up their fees from 10 basis points to like 30.

Pierre Henry: Yeah, and I think there's a whole host of uh investors to your point who will would like to look at this as a defensive type of position. So something that's fully exposed to the volatility, who cares, right? And so if you think about the market neutral side of of what you guys are doing, that to me is uh like, you know, regardless of the direction of the market, you're extracting these inefficiencies. So for an institution that is that's very exciting because uh if the yields fall and and they don't want the volatility risk, I feel like that is a durable compounding type of strategy. So um maybe maybe you can talk more about how that works in depth, you know, whether on the market making side or or or something with uh the the arbitrage that you see across liquidity venues. Just what RE7 does.

Evgeny Gokhberg: Yeah, uh and you're exactly right. It's much easier for anyone in the world to go into a market neutral product that just gives you 15-ish percent, 20% a year very consistently because you're less likely to get fired if uh if you get into that and that's what people care about. So structurally, you know, DeFi yield, um yield farming and all those things, they um everything is very confused. Um sometimes you just, you know, farm points and you gamble away, you do artificial volumes or whatever. But the real function and the essence is actually very fundamental. So when we were raising this um, you know, I was launching this fund, launching this firm and raising money five years ago, I was able to explain to traditional investors who backed us that we're performing a very structural role that banks do in the real economy. What is the function of a bank? It's to channel liquidity into the economy to make sure the wheels of commerce turn. Well, the function of DeFi liquidity providers as a whole, which includes anyone with a wallet, those who are doing it personally or professionally, doesn't really matter, you are, you know, making sure that the spreads are tight. You're making sure that when someone wants to borrow on Kamino or Aave, the cost of debt is acceptable. So when you have, I think this year there's been about 2,000 new DeFi platforms that launched. The only liquidity. Uh and I can tell you as a fact there's not new 2,000 DeFi yield funds out there, not even five that can deploy capital at scale. So the nature of this activity is not arbitrage seeking, it's not farming points, obviously sometimes it's a part of that, but ultimately the core service is there is a supply and demand for capital and there is a price to be paid for this capital to be rented out to various DeFi platforms for their own specific use cases. So then as a liquidity provider, you have to understand the source of risk you're taking. Are you market making coin A and coin B and are you going to take impermanent loss or whatever? If you're lending, you know, how is the margin call system being maintained? If you're underwriting insurance risk or derivatives or or or, you understand the source of financial risk. But ultimately 99% of what we spend our time on is uh smart contract risk assessment. So a few years ago we built a framework on DeFi smart contract risk assessment where we follow the same philosophy as credit where we analyze everything across like 50 different touch points and we say this is, you know, AAA, triple B, triple C. And we construct a portfolio where we want to go to sleep and know that no matter what blows up in the world, we want to wake up to a portfolio that's up for the year. And when you have five years of track delivering that, it makes institutions comfortable talking to you.

Pierre Henry: That makes sense. And I guess the other thing I would wonder is I would look at I would look at the track record of of market neutral funds in '22. Right? And I would say, okay, there are a bunch of guys who because you know you say 20% every year, that's fantastic. But there are a bunch of guys who did fantastically well in '22 until November. And then the whole thing disappeared. And so I guess the question is what is different now in what you're doing versus then? Like what are the key technology improvements or uh ability to deal with counterparty risk, you know, that that makes it uh different today?

Evgeny Gokhberg: Well, you know, it's like the more it changes, the more it stays the same. Like you have a process and you constantly trying to improve your process, make it better, but at the core it's still the same thing. So like the reason why we didn't lose money in '22 was basically twofold. One, things like Terra have never passed our DD process. So we just blacklisted it, we've never put a single cent into Anchor. So then you try to avoid the bad things. And then you also acknowledge that sometimes things will happen that you can never foresee and then you have to make sure that your exposure to them is contained. So for example, you know, we did have some capital on FTX, but the amount was so small that it didn't matter. Um so if you're in a position where no matter what blows up you should be okay anyway, and in the meantime you're doing the best you can to identify the red flags, everything else is just a derivative of that and it's just a process and every day you try to make it better.

Pierre Henry: Yeah, and I think uh, you know, especially with uh Raoul's whole discussion about the hurdle rate we all have um globally of 11%, you're not going to find uh the same safety with that type of ability to meet the hurdle rate outside of what you're doing uh unless you want true directional risk.

Evgeny Gokhberg: And it's diversified because in the real world you lose money when the economy goes down, here or credit blows up, here you lose money when software is hacked. So it's also a very good diversifier for those outside of the crypto world.

Pierre Henry: Yeah, perfect. So we're we're out of time, but I wanted to just let you know that uh it's now the lunch break. So everybody uh it's available I think on all levels and the next session is at 2:20. So if you could thank Evgeny for his uh for his expertise, we're we're very grateful for the conversation.

Evgeny Gokhberg: Thank you.

# Founder’s Update
![Andrew Cronk](https://mlmd2ipi7gdc.i.optimole.com/cb:CxoH.11ca/w:auto/h:auto/q:eco/f:best/https://www.figment.io/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/ajc.png)
Andrew Cronk
CPO & Co-Founder
Published
November 17, 2025
![](https://mlmd2ipi7gdc.i.optimole.com/cb:CxoH.11ca/w:1920/h:1080/q:eco/f:best/https://www.figment.io/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Social-1920x1080-1.jpg)

Share
Copied Link!
Today we announced that Figment isbringing the power of staking to stablecoins, introducing a new way for investors to earn yield.It is a first-of-its-kind combination of staking rewards and hedging against the underlying price volatility of staked tokens. We’re bringing our battle-tested infrastructure and security mindset to our new stablecoin product to offer customers exceptional yield opportunities with the peace of mind of an institutional service.
The first product, OpenTrade Stablecoin Staking Yield Powered by Figment, delivers an average 15% APR on stablecoins (based on historical data and subject to market conditions). The product is powered by a dedicated Figment-run validator combined with OpenTrade’s institution-grade stablecoin yield infrastructure. Crypto.com will serve as the custodian of the staked assets and exchange for transactions.
We methodically started approaching this early in 2025. The mandate was clear: higher yields on stablecoins while mitigating the known risks of lending and DeFi. Figment is bringing its “safety over liveness” approach to deliver higher returns, with lower risk –charting a unique path avoiding the exposure typically associated with DeFi marketplaces.
* The product’s infrastructure has legal protections for institutions not otherwise available in DeFi lending.
* Crypto.com and OpenTrade have an industry-leading agreement that enables SOL tokens to be custodied in a segregated account, over which investors are granted a security interest. Assets are also entirely segregated from assets of the exchange and any other entities.
* Customers work with identified counterparties who are available 24/7.
While investors deposit and withdraw stablecoins, the product’s earnings are derived from Solana (SOL) staking returns generated by Figment and offsetting perpetual SOL futures managed by OpenTrade, together delivering historical returns more than double Solana’s usual \~6.5-7.5% staking reward rate. The product is straightforward, all accessible via the Figment platform and APIs. Deposit stablecoins via Figment, begin earning interest immediately, and withdraw any stablecoin amount at any time.
While the destination of stablecoin yield has been clear, the path to get there is one we have walked very carefully by design. Staking and stablecoins share a stability and predictability to them. Introducing yield infrastructure to deliver higher returns is inherently higher risk, so we focused on the risks institutions can control: security, custody, segregation, counterparties, and legal protections.
More to come in this area as the market evolves and regulatory clarity continues to unfold. OpenTrade Stablecoin Staking Yield Powered by

Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

# **Canary Capital Launches Canary Marinade Solana ETF (SOLC), Giving Investors Simple Access to Solana and Staking Rewards Potential**
Share
*SOLC provides a cost-effective and hands-off way to access price appreciation and staking potential, unlocking a new yield-distributing strategy for a modern portfolio*
BRENTWOOD, Tenn.--([BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))--[Canary Capital Group LLC](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https://www.canary.capital/&esheet=54359654&newsitemid=20251118770018&lan=en-US&anchor=Canary+Capital+Group+LLC&index=1&md5=9bb7478aca5e4beebf53fbb62385830e)(“Canary Capital”), a digital asset investment management firm, today announced the launch of the[Canary Marinade Solana ETF](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https://etfs.canary.capital/solc/&esheet=54359654&newsitemid=20251118770018&lan=en-US&anchor=Canary+Marinade+Solana+ETF&index=2&md5=d79fabdbfe8445597e6b80129e657211)(NASDAQ: SOLC), an exchange-traded product designed to provide investors with spot exposure to Solana (SOL) the native cryptocurrency behind one of the world’s fastest growing

tags.impact_reason: The fireside chat discusses yield generating strategies on Solana and risk management, which is a solid update for the DeFi sector.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a 'Crystalline Smart Contract Module' being analyzed by a robotic arm as the Semantic Anchor. This directly visualizes the core concept of the headline: 'Yield is a Premium on Software Risk'. The crystal represents the valuable, yet fragile, nature of code, while the robotic scanning represents the 'rigorous smart contract auditing' required by institutions. For the homepage text, I integrated a holographic '0.0%' because it diegetically represents the 'market-neutral' and 'fixed income' frameworks Evgeny Gokhberg discusses, which is the key value proposition for TradFi allocators.
visual_idea.description: Inside a cavernous, high-security quantum data vault, a hyper-detailed robotic arm delicately holds a complex, glowing crystalline block representing a smart contract. Precision lasers from the arm scan the block's intricate facets, projecting a subtle, holographic heads-up display that reads '0.0%', signifying a successful market-neutral risk assessment. The background features rows of sleek, obsidian server racks receding into a deep, blue-lit haze, emphasizing institutional scale and security.

In the mid-ground, a hyper-detailed robotic arm, crafted from polished metallic surfaces, delicately cradles a complex, glowing crystalline block, its facets catching and refracting light like intricate crystalline glass. This block visibly represents a smart contract. Precision lasers, emitting from the robotic arm, meticulously scan the block's intricate facets, projecting a subtle, holographic heads-up display floating in the air that distinctly reads '0.0%', signifying a successful market-neutral risk assessment. The background features endless rows of sleek, highly reflective polished obsidian server racks, receding into a deep, volumetric blue-lit haze, emphasizing the institutional scale and security of the facility. Volumetric neon lighting permeates the space, casting soft, blended shadows and brilliant reflections across all surfaces, similar to a dark cyberpunk setting lit by soft neon. The upper portion of the frame features vast negative space, accentuating the vault's immense height. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic contrast, premium editorial photography, high dynamic range, intricate architectural details.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a 'Crystalline Smart Contract Module' being analyzed by a robotic arm as the Semantic Anchor. This directly visualizes the core concept of the headline: 'Yield is a Premium on Software Risk'. The crystal represents the valuable, yet fragile, nature of code, while the robotic scanning represents the 'rigorous smart contract auditing' required by institutions. For the homepage text, I integrated a holographic '0.0%' because it diegetically represents the 'market-neutral' and 'fixed income' frameworks Evgeny Gokhberg discusses, which is the key value proposition for TradFi allocators.

The light refracts and splinters within the block's internal geometric lattice, revealing complex, data-like filaments suspended inside. The background is a soft, out-of-focus bokeh of the larger, dark data vault environment.

A sharp, cool blue laser beam penetrates the highly polished obsidian-like surface of a crystalline smart contract module. The light dramatically refracts, splinters, and expands into a volumetric neon glow within the module's transparent, geometric lattice. Inside, intricate, glowing data-like filaments are suspended, illuminated by the internal light scattering. The module's surface reflects the blue light with the clarity and precision of crystalline glass. The background is a deeply dark, out-of-focus quantum data vault environment, rendered as a soft, ethereal bokeh. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, cinematic contrast, dramatic one-beam lighting.

editorial.headline: RE7 Capital: Institutional Yield is a Premium on Software Risk
editorial.standfirst: Evgeny Gokhberg argues that sustainable DeFi returns now mirror fixed income frameworks, rewarding rigorous smart contract auditing over directional speculation. As ETF products commoditize staking rewards, the true institutional opportunity shifts to market-neutral liquidity provision.
key_takeaways: RE7 Capital frames DeFi yield as a 'software risk premium,' comparable to credit default rates in fixed income portfolios rather than directional market beta.
While Solana staking yields face compression from ETF inflows, the chain's high velocity sustains deep liquidity markets for stablecoins and exotic pairs like JLP.
Operational resilience requires modeling specific L1 vectors, such as Solana chain halts, as liquidity crunch scenarios distinct from traditional counterparty risk.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For institutional allocators, the definition of "yield" in crypto has long been conflated with market beta. **RE7 Capital**, managing a market-neutral DeFi yield fund with a five-year track record, challenges this view, arguing that the real alpha lies in pricing software risk. Speaking at Solana Breakpoint 2025, Managing Partner Evgeny Gokhberg posited that DeFi yields are not merely rewards for volatility, but compensation for underwriting smart contract security. In this framework, a diversified portfolio of liquidity positions functions like a bank's loan book: specific protocols may suffer "defaults" (hacks or exploits), but rigorous auditing ensures the aggregate portfolio outperforms the hurdle rate.

**Solana** presents a unique landscape for this thesis. Unlike EVM chains often utilized for capital preservation, Solana is engineered for velocity and speculation. This high throughput creates distinct yield pockets in lending protocols like **Kamino** or liquidity provider tokens like **JLP**. However, this velocity introduces unique risk vectors. Gokhberg emphasized that risk management on Solana must account for existential infrastructure events, such as chain halts. Unlike a corporate default, a chain halt creates an immediate liquidity crunch where rebalancing becomes impossible. Sophisticated strategies must therefore be designed to withstand periods of forced illiquidity without triggering liquidation cascades.

The launch of products like the **Canary Marinade Solana ETF** signals a commoditization of standard staking yields. As regulatory gates open, the spread on vanilla staking will compress due to fee wars and institutional inflows seeking price appreciation rather than yield. Consequently, the "banking function" of DeFi—providing liquidity to facilitate commerce—remains the defensible moat for active management. By acting as the counterparty in a market-neutral capacity, funds can extract value from market inefficiencies regardless of asset direction, effectively stripping out the volatility that terrifies traditional investment committees.

Ultimately, the institutional adoption of DeFi yield hinges on reframing "crypto risk." It is not about avoiding the next **FTX**—a counterparty failure—but about managing exposure to code. Gokhberg noted that **RE7** avoided the **Terra** collapse in 2022 not by predicting the price crash, but by identifying the algorithmic flaws in **Anchor** during due diligence. For the next wave of capital, success will be defined by this ability to audit the machine, ensuring that when the "software is tested in real time," the portfolio remains solvent.
